comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 7 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://www.usmobile.com/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.usmobile.com%2F) ### Comment > It the same screenshot because only 1 is needed. Can't determine what rule/list is doing it but as seen in screenshot usmobile.com is being modified/blocked and should not be. i have unblocked. But felt this needed to be corrected on your end as this is legitimate site i and others need to be able to reach. ### Screenshots

issue.title: www.pinterest.com
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Ads) [https://www.pinterest.com/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.pinterest.com%2F) ### Comment > So uhh, I got this ad blocker solely for the purpose for trying to remove all, and I mean ALL ads on pinterest as I just have a genuine ick with them, and cannot focus on my work of gathering images and other resources for projects with them. This ad blocker I believe has helped a lot in erasing those ads, however I'm just confused on why some are still able to get by and appear on my feed and whenever I search things up, as on Google, reddit, and other places, this ad blocker is advertised and said by many users to have "solved the entire problem and removed every single ad on pinterest". ### Screenshots

Screenshot 6 ![Screenshot 6](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/iaynqvcg4nk800cc4c0kw4sg4sg4cw05k3kao039bs.jpg?nc=1)

issue.title: Upd #235594 www.cineby.at

comment.body: I would think this rule by itself should be fine `/\/scripts\/[a-z][a-z]\.js$/$script,domain=cineby.at|cineby.*|fmovies.gd|fmovies.*|cineplay.to|cineplay.*`

issue.title: Upd #235594 www.cineby.at

comment.body: @ntnguyen1234 do you think it’s worth keeping `#%#//scriptlet('prevent-addEventListener', '', 'unloaded_at')`? The other rules already block the JS it’s part of, and it's only use I can tell would be as a backup rule.

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).
